question.text: Under Section 13 of PMLA 2002, if the Director finds that a reporting entity has failed to comply with obligations under Chapter IV, the monetary penalty imposed on such entity for each failure shall be:

answer.text: Not less than Rs 5,000 but may extend to Rs 50,000
Not less than Rs 1 lakh but may extend to Rs 5 lakh
Not less than Rs 10,000 but may extend to Rs 1 lakh for each failure
Not less than Rs 25,000 but may extend to Rs 10 lakh

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 13(2)(d) of PMLA 2002 states that the Director may impose a monetary penalty on the reporting entity or its designated director or employees which shall not be less than ten thousand rupees but may extend to one lakh rupees for each failure. **The Trap:** Option B (Rs 1 lakh to Rs 5 lakh) is tempting because candidates may confuse PMLA penalties with SEBI Act penalties under Section 15HB which can extend to Rs 1 crore. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VI (Depository Operations) also covers penalty provisions under PMLA for depository participants. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The PMLA penalty per failure is relatively modest (Rs 10,000 to Rs 1 lakh), but multiple failures can add up significantly — PayPal was fined Rs 96 lakh for multiple violations.
